rationale: The citing paper discusses Nunes and Boatwright (2004) explicitly as prior field evidence using naturalistic anchors and real stakes. It then points out a specific limitation of that work - reliance on a BDM elicitation procedure - to argue that prior evidence is still an incomplete account of anchoring in real markets. This citation helps motivate the paper's core design choice to study anchoring in many pay-what-you-want field transactions.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Nunes and Boatwright (2004) as prior evidence that incidental/naturalistic prices can anchor willingness to pay, while moving beyond its BDM setup. It extends that line by testing anchoring under less contrived, directly consequential payment settings.
